g.text: The new Atari NIO path speaks the same
FujiBus protocol as BBC, MS-DOS, Amiga,
Python, POSIX PTY/TCP, and USB CDC clients.
It does not use the legacy SIO transport.
POSIX emulator: Atari FujiBus over NetSIO
FujiBus bytes over Atari SIO calls
POSIX needs this Atari-specific adapter only because
Altirra exposes SIO through the existing NetSIO custom
device protocol. The core still sees ordinary Channel bytes.
ESP32 hardware: Atari FujiBus over SIO GPIO
queue response for pending SIO R
Current ESP32 Atari NIO selection is:
The ESP32 factory maps SioGpio to
the UartChannel, configures SIO control pins,
Atari SIO W/R/checksum traffic into the raw
FujiBus byte stream consumed by FujiBusTransport.
BBC and MS-DOS do not need a NetSIO/SIO adapter
when they use RS-232/TCP/PTY-style channels. Their
channel is already a raw byte pipe carrying FujiBus.
